basic_details.job_title: UPSSSC Sugarcane Supervisor Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 1182 Posts
basic_details.department: Ganna Adhisthan and Chini Adhisthan, Govt. of Uttar Pradesh
basic_details.short_information: UPSSSC Ganna Paryavekshak Recruitment 2026: Get the latest UPSSSC recruitment notification 2026 for 1182 Ganna Paryavekshak (Sugarcane Supervisor) vacancies under Ganna Adhisthan and Chini Adhisthan, Govt. of Uttar Pradesh. The online application process starts on 7th July 2026 and the last date to apply online is 28th July 2026. Find UPSSSC vacancies across Uttar Pradesh on the official website upsssc.gov.in. You can also get Free Job Alert of "Upcoming UPSSSC Ganna Paryavekshak Recruitment 2026" notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for UPS

important_dates.exam_date: To be notified on UPSSSC website

application_fee.details: Candidates applying for the post of Ganna Paryavekshak are required to pay an online processing fee as specified below. Fee payment is accepted via Credit Card, Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or SBI e-Challan only. No other mode of fee payment is permitted. After successful fee payment/reconciliation, the application form will be automatically submitted and a printout of the application must be taken by the candidate. Fee once paid will not be refunded. The last date for fee reconciliation is 04-08-2026. Examination fee for the Main Examination will be charged separately from shortlisted candidates only, before downloading the admit card. | Note: Dependants of Freedom Fighters, Ex-Servicemen, Women, Divyangjan, and Outstanding Sportspersons pay fee as per their parent/base category (Sl. No. 1 to 4).

age_limit.details: Candidates must be at least 21 years and must not have exceeded 40 years of age as on 1st July of the calendar year in which the Commission advertises the vacancies. Age relaxation is applicable for SC, ST, OBC, PWD (Divyangjan), Ex-Servicemen, Freedom Fighter Dependants, State Government employees, and Chief Minister's Fellowship candidates as per UP Government rules. SC/ST/OBC candidates from UP will get age relaxation as per prevailing UP Government orders.

qualification.details: Only those candidates who appeared in the Preliminary Eligibility Test (PET-2025) and received a valid score card issued by UPSSSC are eligible to apply for this Main Examination. Candidates must possess the required educational qualifications and other eligibility conditions on or before the last date of application (28-07-2026). Shortlisting for the Main Examination will be based on PET-2025 normalized scores - 15 times the number of vacancies (category-wise) up to the cutoff marks will be shortlisted. Candidates with zero/negative absolute or normalized PET-2025 scores will not be shortlisted. | Educational Qualification (Mandatory – as per UP Ganna Paryavekshak Service Rules, 1979)
qualification.domicile: Resident of Uttar Pradesh (reservation benefits only for UP domicile candidates; non-domicile candidates treated as Unreserved)

salary_details.pay_scale: Level-4; Minimum Pay: Rs. 25,500/- | Maximum Pay: Rs. 81,100/-

selection_process: Paper Subject / Part No. of Questions Total Marks Duration
Single Paper (MCQ) Part-1: Crop Science (Fasal Vigyan) 25 25 120 Minutes (2 Hours)
Part-1: Biotechnology, Plant Breeding & Crop Physiology 10 10
Part-1: Soil and Water Conservation (Mrida evam Jal Prabandhan) 15 15
Part-1: Agriculture Economics & Schemes (Krishi Arthashastra evam Yojanaen) 05 05
Part-1: Dairy & Animal Husbandry (Pashupalan evam Dugdha Vigyan) 05 05
Part-2: Knowledge of Computer & IT concepts, Contemporary Technological Development & Innovation in this field 15 15
Part-3: General Information related to the State of Uttar Pradesh 20 20

The selection process for UPSSSC Ganna Paryavekshak Recruitment 2026 consists of a single Written (Main) Examination of 100 marks. There is no interview. Shortlisting for the Written Examination is done on the basis of PET-2025 normalized scores - 15 times the number of category-wise vacancies (up to the cutoff marks
rounded to 2 decimal places) will be shortlisted. The final merit list will be prepared on the basis of marks obtained in the Written Examination. If the exam is held over multiple days/sessions
normalization will be done as per UPSSSC norms published in Mandatory Notice No. 42/05/Vigya0 Anu0 (Gyarah)/2019 dated 22 May 2019.
Note: Negative marking applies - 25% (1/4) of the marks prescribed for a question will be deducted for each wrong answer.

syllabus.details: The Written Examination consists of one question paper with 100 MCQ questions of 1 mark each, divided into 3 parts. All questions are answered in Hindi/English (OMR-based). Negative marking of 1/4 mark applies for each wrong answer. The detailed syllabus as given in the official notification is reproduced below. | Part-1 – Agriculture & Related Subjects (65 Questions, 65 Marks) | Part-2 – Computer & Information Technology (15 Questions, 15 Marks) | Part-3 – General Information Related to the State of Uttar Pradesh (20 Questions, 20 Marks)

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the UPSSSC Online Application System at the official website https://upsssc.gov.in. Hard copy/offline applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. Candidates must login using their PET-2025 Registration Number via OTP sent to their registered mobile/email. All personal information filled during PET-2025 (name, father's name, category, EWS, reservation details, DOB, gender, marital status, contact number, email etc.) will be pre-filled and auto-displayed - these cannot be modified. Candidates must fill in educational qualification details in Section-II of the application. After filling all sections and entering the Verification Code, clicking "Submit" will display the Application Form with an 11-digit registration number - candidates must take a printout and keep it safely. The application form correction window will be open up to 04-08-2026.
Modifications allowed in the application (after final submission, up to 04-08-2026):
Correspondence address
Other personal details such as EWS, horizontal reservation category (DFF, Divyang/PH, Ex-Serviceman, Gender, Outstanding Sportsperson)
Mandatory/additional qualification details filled in the application
Marital status
Documents to be submitted at the time of document verification (after Written Examination result):
Age proof - High School (Madhyamik) certificate/marksheet only (DOB as recorded in High School certificate is final)
Educational qualification marksheets and certificates (Madhyamik onwards)
CCC certificate issued by DOEACC/NIELIT
Caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC) issued from father's side (for UP female candidates)
EWS certificate (for financial year 2025-26, valid for 2026-27; issued between 01-04-2026 and 28-07-2026)
Divyangata (PH/PWD) certificate in prescribed format as per UP Govt. norms (updated format with disability type, sub-category and percentage clearly mentioned)
Freedom Fighter Dependant certificate
Ex-Serviceman certificate (as per prescribed format; candidate should be retired by 28-07-2026)
Outstanding Sportsperson certificate issued by competent authority in prescribed format
NOC from employer (for Government/Semi-Government employees)
Printout of the duly filled online application form
For technical difficulties in online application, candidates may contact UPSSSC at https://upsssc.gov.in or visit the Commission's office on any working day.
Apply Online at: https://upsssc.gov.in
important_instructions: Only candidates who appeared in PET-2025 and hold a valid UPSSSC score card are eligible to apply - others need not apply. Only online applications through the official UPSSSC portal will be accepted; hard copy applications will be rejected. All reserved category candidates of UP must mention their category in the application form; only the most beneficial single relaxation will be granted if multiple categories are claimed. Candidates not domiciled in UP will not be entitled to reservation benefits and will be treated as Unreserved (General). The date of birth as recorded in the High School (Madhyamik) certificate is the only accepted proof; no other document will be accepted. EWS candidates must hold a certificate based on financial year 2025-26 income, valid for FY 2026-27, issued between 01-04-2026 and 28-07-2026; the certificate must be in the prescribed format as per UP Govt. Office Letter No. 0-3/2019/4/1/2002/ka-2/19 T.C.-II dated 14-03-2019. Divyangjan (PH) candidates must ensure their disability certificate is in the updated prescribed format clearly mentioning the disability category/sub-category and percentage, issued by the competent authority as per UP Govt. Office Letter No. 5/2022/18/1/2008/47/ka-2/2022 dated 18-04-2022. For female candidates of UP, only caste certificate issued from the father's side will be accepted. Ex-Servicemen claiming reservation must verify that they possess a certificate as per the prescribed format of the UP Government (GR dated 21 April 2015). Government/Semi-Government employees must obtain an NOC from their employer before participating in the selection process; they must produce it when demanded by the Commission. Married male candidates with more than one living wife, or female candidates who have married a man with an existing living wife, are not eligible - unless exempted by the Governor. If a candidate submits more than one application, only the application with the latest Application Number will be considered; fee paid against other applications will not be adjusted. Mobile phones and electronic gadgets are strictly prohibited inside examination premises. Negative marking of 1/4 mark applies for each wrong answer in the Written Examination. The Commission does not communicate individually with candidates; all information is published on the official website. Candidates must check https://upsssc.gov.in regularly. The Commission's decision on eligibility, selection, and all related matters is final and binding.

content_sections.heading: UPSSSC Sugarcane Supervisor Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: A total of 1182 vacancies have been announced for Ganna Paryavekshak - 1000 posts under General Selection and 182 posts under Special Selection. Vacancies are distributed across Unreserved (UR), Scheduled Caste (SC), Other Backward Class (OBC), and Economically Weaker Section (EWS) categories as per the UP Government's reservation norms. Horizontal reservations are applicable for Women, Ex-Servicemen, Dependants of Freedom Fighters, Persons with Disabilities (PH/Divyangjan), and Outstanding Sportspersons within each category. Saarini-1: Category-wise Vacancy Distribution Divyangata (PH) Sub-Category Classifications applicable: LV (Low Vision) HH (Hard of Hearing) OA (One Arm Affected) OL (One Leg Affected) LC (Leprosy Cured ) Dw (Dwarfism) AAV (Acid Attack Victims)

content_sections.heading: UPSSSC Sugarcane Supervisor Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Only those candidates who appeared in the Preliminary Eligibility Test (PET-2025) and received a valid score card issued by UPSSSC are eligible to apply for this Main Examination. Candidates must possess the required educational qualifications and other eligibility conditions on or before the last date of application (28-07-2026). Shortlisting for the Main Examination will be based on PET-2025 normalized scores - 15 times the number of vacancies (category-wise) up to the cutoff marks will be shortlisted. Candidates with zero/negative absolute or normalized PET-2025 scores will not be shortlisted. Educational Qualification (Mandatory – as per UP Ganna Paryavekshak Service Rules, 1979)

rows.details: Bachelor's Degree (Snataka) in Agricultural Science (Krishi Vigyan) from any university established by law in India
Certificate in Computer Operation (CCC) issued by DOEACC Society (NIELIT)
Valid PET-2025 Score Card issued by UPSSSC - mandatory for all applicants
Resident of Uttar Pradesh (reservation benefits only for UP domicile candidates; non-domicile candidates treated as Unreserved)

content_sections.heading: UPSSSC Sugarcane Supervisor Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit
content_sections.text: Candidates must be at least 21 years and must not have exceeded 40 years of age as on 1st July of the calendar year in which the Commission advertises the vacancies. Age relaxation is applicable for SC, ST, OBC, PWD (Divyangjan), Ex-Servicemen, Freedom Fighter Dependants, State Government employees, and Chief Minister's Fellowship candidates as per UP Government rules. SC/ST/OBC candidates from UP will get age relaxation as per prevailing UP Government orders.

rows.age_limit: Upper age relaxed as per UP Government orders (generally 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC)

content_sections.heading: UPSSSC Sugarcane Supervisor Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for the post of Ganna Paryavekshak are required to pay an online processing fee as specified below. Fee payment is accepted via Credit Card, Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or SBI e-Challan only. No other mode of fee payment is permitted. After successful fee payment/reconciliation, the application form will be automatically submitted and a printout of the application must be taken by the candidate. Fee once paid will not be refunded. The last date for fee reconciliation is 04-08-2026. Examination fee for the Main Examination will be charged separately from shortlisted candidates only, before downloading the admit card. Note: Dependants of Freedom Fighters, Ex-Servicemen, Women, Divyangjan, and Outstanding Sportspersons pay fee as per their parent/base category (Sl. No. 1 to 4).

content_sections.heading: UPSSSC Sugarcane Supervisor Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: The selection process for UPSSSC Ganna Paryavekshak Recruitment 2026 consists of a single Written (Main) Examination of 100 marks. There is no interview. Shortlisting for the Written Examination is done on the basis of PET-2025 normalized scores - 15 times the number of category-wise vacancies (up to the cutoff marks, rounded to 2 decimal places) will be shortlisted. The final merit list will be prepared on the basis of marks obtained in the Written Examination. If the exam is held over multiple days/sessions, normalization will be done as per UPSSSC norms published in Mandatory Notice No. 42/05/Vigya0 Anu0 (Gyarah)/2019 dated 22 May 2019. Written (Main) Examination Plan Note: Negative marking applies - 25% (1/4) of the marks prescribed for a question will be deducted for each wrong answer.

content_sections.heading: UPSSSC Sugarcane Supervisor Recruitment 2026 - Exam Syllabus
content_sections.text: The Written Examination consists of one question paper with 100 MCQ questions of 1 mark each, divided into 3 parts. All questions are answered in Hindi/English (OMR-based). Negative marking of 1/4 mark applies for each wrong answer. The detailed syllabus as given in the official notification is reproduced below. Part-1 – Agriculture & Related Subjects (65 Questions, 65 Marks) Part-2 – Computer & Information Technology (15 Questions, 15 Marks) Part-3 – General Information Related to the State of Uttar Pradesh (20 Questions, 20 Marks)

tables.caption: Part-1 – Agriculture & Related Subjects (65 Questions, 65 Marks)

rows.key_sub_topics: Classification of crops; major cereal, pulse, oilseed, millet, fibre, cash, spice, fodder, fruit, flower, vegetable crops of the state and their production techniques; post-harvest produce management; fruits and vegetables preservation and processing; principles of crop rotation, cropping patterns, dry farming, integrated farming, natural farming, mono cropping, multiple cropping, organic farming; seed importance, types and production; seed treatment; principles of crop protection, major equipment and chemicals, pest management; insect pests and diseases of major crops; principles and systems of produce storage; factors affecting quality of produce and its management.
Importance and utility of biotechnology in agriculture; principles, use and importance of genetics and plant breeding; photosynthesis, respiration, evaporation, plant anatomy and metabolism.

rows.topic: Soil and Water Conservation (Mrida evam Jal Prabandhan)
rows.key_sub_topics: Properties and components of soil; process of soil formation; types of soil erosion and conservation techniques; essential nutrients found in soil, role and deficiency symptoms; classification of fertilizers, nutrient content and application methods; integrated nutrient management, types, importance and role of organic fertilizers/bio-fertilizers; soil microbiology; soil survey, soil sampling methods and soil conservation techniques; source, method and importance of irrigation; basic concepts and methods of drainage; watershed management; types of pollution and environmental protection.

Principles of agriculture extension and rural development; extension methodologies, audio-visual aids, their classification and importance; training objectives, importance and types; different programs and schemes of the Govt. of India and State Govt. for farmers' welfare and rural development.

Role of agriculture in one trillion economy of UP; economic reforms in agriculture sector; concept of import and export of agricultural produce; formulation of plans for agriculture; principles of agricultural statistics; principles and importance of agricultural economics; MSP of different crops; agricultural marketing.

rows.topic: Dairy & Animal Husbandry (Pashupalan evam Dugdha Vigyan)
rows.key_sub_topics: Different breeds of domestic animals and feed management; objectives and methods of animal breeding; milk production and its distribution; basic knowledge of poultry farming, fish farming, apiculture and sericulture; major diseases of domestic animals, diagnosis and treatment.

tables.caption: Part-2 – Computer & Information Technology (15 Questions, 15 Marks)

rows.topic: Knowledge of Concepts of Computer and Information Technology and Contemporary Technological Development and Innovation in this field
rows.key_sub_topics: History, introduction and application of Computer, IT, Internet and WWW; General knowledge of hardware and software, input and output, Internet protocol/IP address, IT gadgets and their application, creation of email ID and use/operation, operation of printer, tablet and mobile; important elements of Word Processing (MS-Word) and Excel Processing (MS-Excel); Operating System, Social Networking, e-Governance; Digital financial tools and applications; Future skills and Cyber Security; Technological development and innovation in the field of Computer and IT (Artificial Intelligence, Big Data Processing, Deep Learning, Machine Learning, Internet of Things) and India's achievements in this field.

tables.caption: Part-3 – General Information Related to the State of Uttar Pradesh (20 Questions, 20 Marks)

rows.key_sub_topics: History, culture, art, architecture, festivals, folk dance, literature, regional languages, heritage, social customs and tourism; geographical landscape and environment; natural resources, climate, soil, forest, wildlife, mines and minerals; economy, agriculture, industry, business and employment; polity, administration of Uttar Pradesh; current events and achievements of Uttar Pradesh State in various fields.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for UPSSSC Sugarcane Supervisor Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Applications must be submitted online only through the UPSSSC Online Application System at the official website https://upsssc.gov.in. Hard copy/offline applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. Candidates must login using their PET-2025 Registration Number via OTP sent to their registered mobile/email. All personal information filled during PET-2025 (name, father's name, category, EWS, reservation details, DOB, gender, marital status, contact number, email etc.) will be pre-filled and auto-displayed - these cannot be modified. faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in UPSSSC Ganna Paryavekshak Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: UPSSSC has announced a total of 1182 vacancies for Ganna Paryavekshak (Sugarcane Supervisor), including 1000 posts under General Selection and 182 posts under Special Selection.

faq.question: Q2. What are the important dates for UPSSSC Sugarcane Supervisor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application starts on 7 July 2026 and the last date to apply is 28 July 2026. Candidates can make corrections in the form until 4 August 2026.

faq.question: Q3. Who is eligible to apply for UPSSSC Ganna Paryavekshak 2026?
faq.answer: Only candidates who have appeared in PET-2025 and possess a valid scorecard are eligible. Additionally, candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree in Agriculture and CCC certificate.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for UPSSSC Sugarcane Supervisor Vacancy 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 21 years and the maximum age is 40 years as of 1 July 2026. Age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories as per UP Government rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary of a Ganna Paryavekshak in UPSSSC?
faq.answer: The salary is under Pay Level-4, with a pay scale of ₹25,500 to ₹81,100 per month, along with additional allowances.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for UPSSSC Ganna Paryavekshak Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: All candidates need to pay only a processing fee of ₹25, while the examination fee will be charged later for shortlisted candidates.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for UPSSSC Sugarcane Supervisor 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process includes a single written (main) examination of 100 marks. Candidates are shortlisted based on PET-2025 scores, and there is no interview.

faq.question: Q8. What is the exam pattern for UPSSSC Ganna Paryavekshak Main Exam 2026?
faq.answer: The exam consists of 100 MCQ questions (100 marks) with a duration of 2 hours. There is a negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer.

faq.question: Q9. How can I apply online for UPSSSC Ganna Paryavekshak Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through the official website upsssc.gov.in by logging in using their PET-2025 registration number and completing the application form.

faq.question: Q10. Is CCC certificate mandatory for UPSSSC Ganna Paryavekshak 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, a CCC certificate issued by NIELIT (DOEACC) is mandatory along with an Agriculture degree to be eligible for the post.
